mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production from recession trough signals improving supply conditions and corporate earnings, leading to positive forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO value 95.9709 (July 2009) up 0.5% from prior month
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases over three consecutive months and SPX 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of such occurrences, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 9% indicates severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of observations where UNRATE > 9%, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized demand recovery, amplifying positive equity returns over a one-month horizon through consumption and investment channels.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST value 582 (June 2009) -1.2% m/m, but trough expected
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month but SPX 20-day return is negative in more than 40% of instances, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero-lower-bound regime where FEDFUNDS cannot be lowered further, rising consumer prices signal reflation and diminished deflation risk, which boosts equity prices over the following month.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL value 214.469, -2.1% y/y but m/m change may turn positive
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL m/m rises while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and SPX 20-day return is negative in more than half the cases, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A transition from contraction to positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth marks the end of recession and signals improving corporate earnings, leading to positive quarterly equity returns.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1 value 12892.4 (Q2 2009) represents a ~1% annualized decline but prior Q1 was steeper, indicating potential trough
failure_condition: If GDPC1 qoq growth turns positive and SPX 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such quarters, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates that labor market improvement is translating into consumer spending, a powerful combination for positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS value 131488 (July 2009) -247k decline, but improving from larger losses
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month over a 3-month period and SPX 20-day return is negative more than 50% of the time, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the CPI signals the end of deflationary fears, reducing the real debt burden and boosting short-term equity prices over the next week.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL value 214.469 (July 2009) -0.2% m/m, but prior months were flat, turning positive expected
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL m/m is positive and SPX 5-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of observations, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During the early expansion phase following a recession, industrial production growth is a particularly strong leading indicator of corporate profits and equity returns, outperforming other phases.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO value 95.9709 (July 2009), up from cyclical low
fact: NBER dated recession trough June 2009
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises month-over-month during the first 12 months after recession trough and SPX 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences, hypothesis fails.
